questions.question: A patient's white blood cell count increases from $250$ cells/$mu$L to $375$ cells/$mu$L. What is the percent increase?
A medicine dosage is $25%$ effective in treating a disease. If $80$ patients are treated, how many are expected to recover?
The rate of cell division in a Petri dish decreases by $18%$ per hour. If there are $1200$ cells initially, how many cells will be left after $2$ hours?
A hospital has a $22%$ occupancy rate for its ICU beds. If there are $50$ beds, how many are occupied?
A pharmaceutical company claims its new drug is $35%$ more effective than the existing treatment. If the existing treatment is $40%$ effective, what is the percent effectiveness of the new drug?
A medical researcher collects data on the effectiveness of a vaccine. The results show that $280$ out of $400$ participants develop immunity. What percent of participants develop immunity?
The concentration of a solution is $15%$ acid. If $24$ liters of the solution contain $x$ liters of acid, what is the value of $x$?
A doctor prescribes a $12%$ iodine solution to a patient. If the patient needs $0.5$ liters of the solution, how many milliliters of pure iodine will the patient receive?
A population of bacteria grows at a rate of $8%$ per day. If there are initially $5000$ bacteria, how many will there be after $10$ days? Show your work.

A clinical trial tests the effectiveness of a new medication. The results show that $75%$ of patients experience relief from symptoms. If $40$ patients are treated, how many are expected to experience relief? Calculate the percent error if the actual number of patients experiencing relief is $32$.

teacher_rules: Emphasize the importance of converting between percentages and decimals
Remind students to consider the context when calculating percent increase or decrease
Encourage students to check their work by plugging in values
